identity.name: Onboarding Checklist
identity.definition: An Onboarding Checklist is a system-generated, user-specific sequence of setup and discovery tasks that appears after a user's first login to Renaissance Intelligence.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How an Onboarding Checklist appears as a dashboard widget

attributes.name: Role Variant
attributes.description: Whether this is a teacher or admin checklist.

attributes.name: Progress
attributes.description: Percentage of steps completed or skipped (0-100).

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Overall checklist state: not-started, in-progress, complete, or dismissed.

attributes.name: Steps
attributes.description: The ordered set of steps in this checklist.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How an Onboarding Checklist appears in admin views

attributes.name: User
attributes.description: The teacher or admin this checklist belongs to.

attributes.name: Role Variant
attributes.description: Whether this is a teacher or admin checklist.

attributes.name: Progress
attributes.description: Percentage of steps completed or skipped.

representations.heading: Detail layout
representations.description: Full expanded Onboarding Checklist with all steps

attributes.name: User
attributes.description: The teacher or admin this checklist belongs to.

attributes.name: Role Variant
attributes.description: teacher or admin — determines which steps appear.

attributes.name: Progress
attributes.description: Percentage of steps completed or skipped (0-100).

attributes.name: Steps
attributes.description: The ordered set of steps in this checklist.

attributes.name: Created Date
attributes.description: When the checklist was generated (user's first login).

attributes.name: Completed Date
attributes.description: When all steps were completed or skipped.

attributes.name: Dismissed Date
attributes.description: When the user dismissed the checklist.

attributes.name: Days Since Start
attributes.description: Calculated field: days elapsed since the checklist was created.

stories.title: First Login Onboarding
stories.benefit: I know exactly what setup steps to take and can track my progress toward being fully onboarded.
stories.whenClause: I log in to Renaissance Intelligence for the first time,
stories.thenClause: a personalized checklist appears on my dashboard showing the steps I need to complete, with progress tracking and direct links to each action.

stories.title: Monitor Teacher Onboarding
stories.benefit: I can identify which teachers need onboarding support and intervene before they stall.
stories.whenClause: I view the admin onboarding dashboard,
stories.thenClause: I see a list of teachers with their checklist progress, days since start, and current status so I can prioritize outreach.

businessRules.title: Auto-Generation
businessRules.description: A checklist is automatically created for every user upon their first login. The step set is determined by the user's role (teacher vs. admin).

businessRules.title: One Per User
businessRules.description: Each user gets exactly one Onboarding Checklist. It cannot be duplicated, and re-login does not create a new one.

businessRules.title: Progress Calculation
businessRules.description: Progress = (completed steps + skipped steps) / total steps * 100. Both completed and skipped steps count toward progress.

businessRules.title: Auto-Completion
businessRules.description: When all steps reach a terminal state (complete or skipped), the checklist status automatically transitions to complete.

businessRules.title: Dismissal
businessRules.description: Users can dismiss the checklist widget from their dashboard. Dismissal hides it from view but the checklist data persists for admin reporting.

businessRules.title: Role-Based Step Sets
businessRules.description: Teacher checklists typically have 5 steps focused on classroom setup. Admin checklists have 8 steps including school-level configuration tasks.

businessRules.title: Staleness Detection
businessRules.description: If daysSinceStart exceeds a threshold (e.g., 14 days) and progress is below 50%, the checklist may trigger an admin notification.

businessRules.title: Reset Capability
businessRules.description: Only admins can reset a teacher's checklist, which returns all steps to not-started and resets progress to 0%. This is used for re-training scenarios.

states.name: Not Started
states.description: Checklist has been generated but no steps attempted
states.triggers: User's first login generates the checklist

states.name: In Progress
states.description: At least one step has been started, completed, or skipped

states.name: Complete
states.description: All steps are in a terminal state (complete or skipped)

states.name: Dismissed
states.description: User has dismissed the checklist from their dashboard

relationships.mechanics: A checklist contains an ordered set of steps. Step status changes propagate to recalculate checklist progress and potentially trigger checklist completion.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Checklist contains 5-8 Steps; each Step belongs to exactly one Checklist.
relationships.sorts: Steps within a checklist: by order (ascending, fixed).
relationships.filters: By step status (not-started, in-progress, complete, skipped).
relationships.dependencies: If a checklist is reset, all its steps return to not-started.

relationships.mechanics: Each checklist belongs to exactly one teacher/admin user. Generated automatically on first login based on the user's role.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-one. Each Teacher has exactly one Checklist; each Checklist belongs to one Teacher.
relationships.sorts: Checklists across teachers: by progress (ascending for admins to find stragglers), by days since start.
relationships.filters: By role variant (teacher vs. admin), by status, by progress range.
relationships.dependencies: If a teacher is deactivated, the checklist data is retained for historical reporting.

relationships.mechanics: Steps within a checklist may link to Educator Academy Modules for supplementary training. Module completion can auto-complete associated steps.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (indirect, through steps). A Checklist's steps may reference multiple Modules; a Module can be linked from steps in multiple Checklists.
relationships.dependencies: Module availability affects step help links but not checklist structure.

nestedObjects.name: Onboarding Step
nestedObjects.description: Ordered sequence of actionable setup tasks within this checklist

allAttributes.name: Checklist ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for this checklist instance.

allAttributes.name: User
allAttributes.description: The teacher or admin this checklist belongs to.

allAttributes.name: Role Variant
allAttributes.description: Determines the step set: teacher (5 steps) or admin (8 steps).

allAttributes.name: Progress
allAttributes.description: Percentage of steps in terminal state (complete + skipped) / total. 0-100.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Overall state: not-started, in-progress, complete, or dismissed.

allAttributes.name: Steps
allAttributes.description: Ordered array of step references composing this checklist.

allAttributes.name: Created Date
allAttributes.description: Timestamp of checklist generation (user's first login).

allAttributes.name: Completed Date
allAttributes.description: Timestamp when checklist reached 100% progress. Null if not complete.

allAttributes.name: Dismissed Date
allAttributes.description: Timestamp when the user dismissed the checklist widget. Null if not dismissed.

allAttributes.name: Days Since Start
allAttributes.description: Calculated field: calendar days since Created Date. Used for staleness detection.

structure.evidence: An Onboarding Checklist has structured attributes: ID, user reference, role variant, progress percentage, status, step references, created/completed/dismissed dates, and days since start. You can design a detail page for a Checklist.

instances.evidence: Ms. Johnson's teacher checklist (40% progress, 3 days in), Dr. Smith's admin checklist (100% complete, 14 days), Mr. Garcia's teacher checklist (60% progress, dismissed after 7 days). Every user gets a unique Checklist instance.

purpose.evidence: Checklists drive user activation by providing guided setup workflows. Teachers use them to complete first-time configuration. Admins monitor them to identify teachers who need onboarding support. Completion data feeds activation metrics.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain container object. User-specific onboarding workflow that contains ordered steps and tracks activation progress.

synonyms.notes: Emphasizes the guided nature; Checklist emphasizes progress tracking
The section name where the checklist appears on the dashboard
Emphasizes the first-login trigger; used in CS team documentation
Emphasizes the business goal of user activation; internal analytics term

shapeshifterMatrix.description: The onboarding checklist shown as a dashboard widget with progress and next steps. New users see what they need to complete and pick up where they left off.
The complete onboarding checklist — all steps, progress, and management controls. Users work through setup tasks; admins can reset or review completion status.
Onboarding checklists listed in an admin table with progress, status, and days since start. Admins monitor which users are completing onboarding and who's stalled.
